article_title: Crunchyroll Confirms Data Breach Exposing 6.8 Million Users After Hacker Compromises Telus Outsourcing Partner

content_preview.title: Crunchyroll Confirms Data Breach Exposing 6.8 Million Users After Hacker Compromises Telus Outsourcing Partner
content_preview.summary: Sony's anime streaming service confirmed a breach of customer service ticket data after a threat actor compromised a Telus International support agent's credentials, claiming to have stolen 100 GB of user data.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Crunchyroll, the Sony-owned anime streaming service with over 17 million paid subscribers, confirmed on March 24 that it is investigating a data breach involving customer service ticket information after a threat actor claimed to have exfiltrated approximately 100 GB of user data. The breach, which occurred on March 12, was traced to a compromised employee at Telus International, the business process outsourcing firm that handles Crunchyroll's customer support operations, according ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear breach article with good supply-chain context. 539 words for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: BleepingComputer confirmed: 6.8M users, 8M tickets, Telus International, Okta SSO, March 12, $5M extortion, data types. TechCrunch confirmed official acknowledgment.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Crunchyroll coverage. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid breach article with verified claims. Ready for publication.
